alternatives: Construct business dependencies lazily from UIKit lifecycle callbacks
consequences: Rendering shares presentation semantics with SwiftUI
Dependency lifetime stays visible in composition code
context: UIViewController is both a framework lifecycle object and a rendering adapter, so its presentation and composition responsibilities need an explicit boundary.
decision: Receive immutable display-ready state through a display port, forward typed events, mutate UI on MainActor, and construct business dependencies only in Builders or composition roots.
migration: Introduce a display port, move presentation mapping out of UIViewController, isolate UI mutation to MainActor, and move dependency construction to composition code.
title: UIKit rendering adapter
verification_impact: The final joined AI consistency review confirms UIKit rendering, lifecycle, isolation, and dependency guidance agrees with shared presentation Rules.
